reasoning: Arsenal won the first leg of their Champions League quarter-final against Sporting CP 1-0 away in Lisbon, courtesy of a stoppage-time goal from Kai Havertz. Heading into the second leg at home (the Emirates) with a 1-0 aggregate lead, Arsenal is mathematically and practically a massive favorite to advance to the semi-final. The Polymarket price as of April 11, 2026, is 0.935. While there are some injury and fitness questions surrounding key players (Saka, Timber, and an in-game withdrawal of Odegaard during the first leg), the market probability strongly incorporates this information. Since Arsenal is heavily favored due to both their aggregate lead and their home advantage in the decisive leg, a probability closely tracking the market (around 93-94%) is standard. I've scaled the numbers slightly down in accordance with the requested formatting constraint (uncertainty increasing with horizon).
